fault_hypothesis: Lines 80-81 incorrectly pop the last element when both streams end with the same version, but the spec requires both copies to be kept. This post-processing block should not exist.

constraints.intent: The pop() call at line 81 should never execute; it incorrectly removes a duplicate that must be kept.
After each merge iteration, the merged list length must equal the total number of elements consumed from both streams.
After the conditional pop, the merged length should still equal the sum of both inputs; if it is one less, the pop was wrong.
The merged result must always contain exactly as many elements as the sum of both input streams.
